title: The State of Generative Media 2026
body: Industry report on enterprise AI adoption
Published 2026

title: Agenda
body: Enterprise model deployment landscape
Market concentration in LLM providers
Infrastructure selection criteria
Model performance benchmarks
Release cycle dynamics

title: Enterprise Model Deployment Scale
body: Enterprise production deployments use a median of 14 different models
Organizations maintain diverse model portfolios in production environments
Multi-model strategies are standard practice across enterprises

title: LLM Market Concentration
body: OpenAI, Gemini, and Anthropic together command 89% of enterprise wallet share in LLMs
Three major providers dominate enterprise spending on language models
Market consolidation around leading AI vendors

title: Primary Selection Criterion: Cost
body: In a survey with Artificial Analysis, 58% of organizations identified cost optimization as their primary criterion when selecting model infrastructure
Cost considerations outweigh other factors for majority of enterprises
Economic efficiency drives infrastructure decisions

title: Secondary Selection Factors
body: 42% of organizations prioritize criteria beyond cost optimization
Performance, reliability, and integration capabilities influence remaining decisions
Survey conducted in partnership with Artificial Analysis

title: Benchmark Leadership: Seedance 1.0
body: Seedance 1.0 topped leaderboards in June 2025
New entrant achieved top performance rankings mid-year
Leaderboard positions reflect competitive model landscape

title: Model Release Frequency
body: Model releases in 2025 came every 4-6 weeks
Rapid iteration cycle across the industry
High-frequency release schedule standard in 2025

title: Impact on Enterprise Teams
body: Model releases in 2025 came every 4-6 weeks, keeping teams in constant evaluation mode
Continuous assessment burden on enterprise AI teams
Evaluation processes must adapt to rapid release cycles

title: Multi-Model Strategy Drivers
body: Median deployment of 14 different models reflects specialized use cases
No single model optimizes for all enterprise requirements
Portfolio approach balances cost, performance, and capability needs

title: Market Dynamics: Provider Competition
body: 89% wallet share concentration among three providers indicates competitive barriers
OpenAI, Gemini, and Anthropic maintain dominant market positions
Remaining 11% distributed across alternative providers

title: Evaluation Cycle Challenges
body: 4-6 week release intervals create perpetual evaluation workload
Teams balance staying current with operational stability
Constant assessment mode impacts resource allocation

title: Summary and Outlook
body: Enterprise AI characterized by multi-model deployments and cost-driven decisions
Market consolidation coexists with rapid innovation cycles
Organizations navigate trade-offs between evaluation burden and competitive advantage
